decisions.title: Prioritize Canvas V2 features vs Edge capabilities in Q2
decisions.context: Heavy Canvas feature load (disposition, versioning, markdown, pass/fail) competing with Edge stress testing and sync improvements. Both critical to 'Nail the Core' and 'Embed Deeper' pillars.

arguments.for: Canvas drives decision capture (North Star) and has 6+ features in flight. Customer feedback on sync panels shows demand.
arguments.against: Edge benchmarking (ENG-10530) and Parquet pipeline (ENG-10529) are foundational for 'Build the Moat'. Delaying risks competitive position.

decisions.title: Address Critical CVEs immediately or defer non-customer-facing systems
decisions.context: ENG-10521 flagged today (4/20). Security posture impacts enterprise sales. ALB/ELB configs (ENG-10260, ENG-10261) also open.

arguments.for: Security table stakes for 'Embed Deeper' in regulated industries. Fast fix shows operational maturity.
arguments.against: If non-customer-facing, defer to maintain Canvas/Edge velocity. Over-rotation on infra slows product momentum.

decisions.title: Commit to Canvas execution/approval UX scope for May release
decisions.context: DES-189 underway but undefined scope. Pass/Fail functions (ENG-9232) and disposition history (ENG-9828) are dependencies. Unclear if this is MVP or full workflow.

arguments.for: Execution/approval is core to 'Decision' in Signal>Evidence>Decision. Drives North Star metric directly.
arguments.against: Scope creep risk. Better to ship smaller, iterate. Versioning (ENG-9441, ENG-9442) may be sufficient for initial decision tracking.

decisions.title: Standardize calculated channel handling to prevent performance issues
decisions.context: ENG-10185 (combinatoric explosion) and ENG-8512 (duplicate names) show systemic design debt. Impacts Explore V2 performance.

arguments.for: Core stability issue. Calculated channels are foundation for Python Rules and Ask SIFT. Fixing now prevents future escalations.
arguments.against: Lower urgency than customer-facing features. Can be bandaided with sampling method defaults (ENG-10497) short-term.

decisions.title: Define Edge-to-Sift sync roadmap: GUI-first or API-first
decisions.context: ENG-10355/10356 add GUI features (units, enums, search). ENG-10529 is backend Parquet pipeline. Unclear if GUI is customer demo tool or production interface.

arguments.for: GUI accelerates sales demos and onboarding ('Embed Deeper'). Search (ENG-10376) is high-value UX win.
arguments.against: API-first scales better for enterprise customers with existing tooling. GUI may be throwaway effort if customers integrate headlessly.

timeAudit.weekSummary: High meeting load (80% of work week). Canvas and Edge syncs consuming 6hrs. 1:1s well-distributed (3 today). Security incident added unplanned 1hr. Focus blocks fragmented (2x 1hr vs consolidated deep work).
alerts: Zero 2+ hour focus blocks this week - strategic work at risk
Security incident displaced planned Canvas roadmap review
Back-to-back 1:1s 11:30-17:00 Wed - no buffer for follow-up
Edge and Canvas syncs could be consolidated (similar stakeholders)
